meta.section: Identity & Access — Authentication Methods (part 1 of 2: methods + strength)
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/entra) — portal paths, setting labels, and defaults grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: All of these live at Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods (the one exception, the custom strength, is under Protection > Authentication strengths). The pattern for every method is the same: open the method > Enable = Yes/No > Include target = All users > Save.

Legacy per-user MFA and SSPR method management was deprecated on 2025-09-30 — methods are now governed only by the Authentication methods policy. Confirm the policy's migration state is 'Migration Complete' (Authentication methods > Policies > Manage migration) so legacy settings are ignored.

Before DISABLING any phone-based method (SMS, Voice), audit how many users have that as their ONLY registered method (Authentication methods > Activity / registration report) and get them onto Authenticator or a passkey first — otherwise they'll be locked out.
Requires the Authentication Policy Administrator role (or higher). Changes can take time to apply to users.

requiredSettings.label: Enable and Target
requiredSettings.value: Enable = Yes; Include = All users; Authentication mode = Any

requiredSettings.label: Configure — Show application name in notifications
requiredSettings.value: Enabled

requiredSettings.label: Configure — Show geographic location in notifications
requiredSettings.value: Enabled

requiredSettings.label: Number matching
requiredSettings.value: On (now default/enforced for push by Microsoft)

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Microsoft Authenticator.
Basics: Enable = Yes, Target = All users, Authentication mode = Any.
Configure tab: set 'Show application name in push and passwordless notifications' = Enabled, and 'Show geographic location…' = Enabled.
Save.

requiredSettings.label: Registration campaign state
requiredSettings.value: Enabled (Microsoft-managed default is also Enabled)

requiredSettings.label: Authentication method to nudge
requiredSettings.value: Microsoft Authenticator

requiredSettings.label: Days allowed to snooze
requiredSettings.value: 1 (users can snooze once per day)

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Registration campaign.
Set State = Enabled and Authentication method = Microsoft Authenticator; Target = All users (exclude break-glass accounts).
Set Days allowed to snooze = 1 (optionally enable 'Limited number of snoozes' to force setup after 3 skips).
Save.

requiredSettings.label: How MFA is actually enforced
requiredSettings.value: Via the Conditional Access 'Require MFA for Internal Users' policy (see the CA guidance) with Security Defaults turned OFF — there is no single 'require MFA for all' toggle in the Authentication methods blade

requiredSettings.label: Authentication methods > Settings — System-preferred MFA
requiredSettings.value: Enabled (prompts users for their most secure registered method)

requiredSettings.label: Authentication methods > Settings — Report suspicious activity
requiredSettings.value: Enabled (lets users flag rogue MFA prompts; feeds Identity Protection)

steps: Tenant-wide MFA enforcement is delivered by Conditional Access, not here — confirm the CA MFA policies are On and Security Defaults is Disabled (see DefSecSettings).
In Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Settings: set System-preferred MFA = Enabled and Report suspicious activity = Enabled (both target All users).
Save.
Note (HIGH impact): this is the backbone that requires all users to complete MFA — validate the CA policy in Report-only first and exclude break-glass accounts.

requiredSettings.label: Enable and Target
requiredSettings.value: Enable = Yes; Include = All users

requiredSettings.label: Use
requiredSettings.value: One-time, time-limited passcode for onboarding / passwordless setup / account recovery (configurable lifetime, one-time vs reusable)

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Temporary Access Pass.
Enable = Yes, Target = All users; review the lifetime / one-time-use settings on the Configure tab.
Save.

requiredSettings.label: Enable
requiredSettings.value: No — disable SMS text message as an authentication method

requiredSettings.label: Pre-deploy audit
requiredSettings.value: Find users whose ONLY registered method is SMS and migrate them to Authenticator/passkey first

steps: Audit first: Authentication methods > Activity (or the registration report) for users with SMS as their sole method.
Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > SMS.
Set Enable = No, Save.
Note (HIGH impact): anyone relying solely on SMS will be unable to complete MFA until they register a stronger method — communicate and remediate before flipping.

requiredSettings.label: Enable
requiredSettings.value: No — disable voice call as an authentication method

requiredSettings.label: Pre-deploy audit
requiredSettings.value: Find users whose ONLY registered method is voice and migrate them first

steps: Audit first for users with voice call as their sole method.
Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Voice call.
Set Enable = No, Save.
Note (HIGH impact): same lockout risk as SMS — remediate affected users before disabling.

requiredSettings.label: Enable and Target
requiredSettings.value: Enable = Yes; Include = All users — allows third-party TOTP authenticator apps as a valid method

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Third-party software OATH tokens.
Enable = Yes, Target = All users.
Save.

requiredSettings.label: Enable
requiredSettings.value: No — disable email one-time passcode as an authentication method

requiredSettings.label: Scope note
requiredSettings.value: Email OTP is mainly a B2B guest fallback; disabling affects guest redemption, not typically member sign-in

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Email OTP.
Set Enable = No, Save.
Note: if the tenant collaborates with external guests who redeem via email OTP, confirm an alternative (Microsoft account / Entra) works for them first.

requiredSettings.label: Enable and Target
requiredSettings.value: Enable = Yes; Include = All users — allows physical OATH-TOTP hardware tokens

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Hardware OATH tokens (Preview).
Enable = Yes, Target = All users; upload/assign tokens as needed.
Save.

requiredSettings.label: Enable and Target
requiredSettings.value: Enable = Yes; Include = All users (covers FIDO2 keys and passkeys)

requiredSettings.label: Allow self-service set up
requiredSettings.value: Yes

requiredSettings.label: Enforce attestation
requiredSettings.value: Yes

requiredSettings.label: Enforce key restrictions
requiredSettings.value: Yes — restrict to the approved AAGUIDs (approved key models only)

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > FIDO2 security key.
Basics: Enable = Yes, Target = All users.
Configure tab: Allow self-service set up = Yes; Enforce attestation = Yes; Enforce key restrictions = Yes and add the approved AAGUIDs.
Save.

requiredSettings.label: Enable
requiredSettings.value: No — disable QR code (PIN) authentication (a preview method)

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > QR code (Preview).
Set Enable = No, Save.

requiredSettings.label: Lockout threshold
requiredSettings.value: 10 (failed sign-ins before first lockout) — 10 or lower is the recommendation

requiredSettings.label: Lockout duration in seconds
requiredSettings.value: 60 (or higher)

requiredSettings.label: Custom banned password list
requiredSettings.value: Enforce = Yes; add org-specific weak terms (company name, local sports teams, seasons, etc.)

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Password protection.
Set Lockout threshold = 10 and Lockout duration = 60 seconds.
Set 'Enforce custom list' = Yes and populate the custom banned password list (the global banned list is always on).
Save. Note: customizing smart-lockout values requires Entra ID P1 (included with Business Premium).

requiredSettings.label: Enable
requiredSettings.value: Disabled in this baseline (framework only) — turn on only if the org uses smart cards / X.509 certificate sign-in

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication methods > Certificate-based authentication.
Confirm Enable = No for the baseline (no CBA in use).
If the client later adopts smart cards: upload the trusted CAs (Protection > Show more > Certificate authorities), then enable and configure the authentication binding (single- vs multi-factor).

requiredSettings.label: Type
requiredSettings.value: Custom authentication strength

requiredSettings.label: Allowed method combinations
requiredSettings.value: Phishing-resistant / strong MFA: FIDO2 security key, Windows Hello for Business, Certificate-based auth (multifactor), Microsoft Authenticator (phone sign-in), hardware OATH — per the baseline

requiredSettings.label: Consumed by
requiredSettings.value: The Conditional Access admin-MFA policy references this strength (see the CA guidance)

steps: Entra ID > Protection > Authentication strengths > New authentication strength.
Name it (e.g. 'Inforcer Authentication Strength'), then tick the allowed method combinations for the baseline (phishing-resistant + strong methods).
Save. It then appears as a Grant control option ('Require authentication strength') in Conditional Access.
Note: this is the strength the admin CA policy points at — build/verify it before that CA policy is switched On.
